terms.definition: The default claim assumed until sample evidence contradicts it—often "no effect" or "parameter equals a specified value."
The claim accepted when H₀ is rejected; specifies the direction or form of effect the test is designed to detect.
Probability, assuming H₀ is true, of observing a test statistic at least as extreme as the one calculated from the data.
Pre-specified maximum Type I error rate; H₀ is rejected when p-value ≤ α.
Rejecting H₀ when H₀ is actually true—a false positive; probability α under the chosen decision rule.
Failing to reject H₀ when H₀ is actually false—a false negative; probability β.
Probability 1 − β of correctly rejecting H₀ when it is false; depends on sample size, effect size, and variability.
A standardized function of sample data (e.g. t, z) whose distribution under H₀ is used to compute the p-value.
Test comparing sample mean x̄ to hypothesized μ₀ using t = (x̄ − μ₀)/(s/√n) when population variance is unknown.
